LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SEIU LOCAL 87'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. CONTINUED TO MARCH 8, 2019. DEFENDANT HAS LEAVE TO FILE A BRIEF OF NO MORE THAN 5 PAGES EXPLAINING WHETHER SECTION 301 OF THE LMRA PREEMPTS PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS. PLEASE FOCUS ON SECTION 301 CASELAW THAT INVOLVES FEHA, CFRA AND/OR FMLA CLAIMS AS MOST CASES HAVE NOT FOUND PREEMPTION IN THE FEHA CONTEXT. (SEE RAMIREZ V. FOX TELEVISION STATION, INC. (1993) 998 F.2D 743, 748.) DEFENDANT'S BRIEF SHALL BE FILED AND SERVED BY FEBRUARY 27, 2019. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO FILE A RESPONDING BRIEF OF NO MORE THAN 5 PAGES BY MARCH 4, 2019. ALL PARTIES TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES OF THEIR FILINGS WITH DEPARTMENT 302. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR# 8600 EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/RBU)

NOTICE OF TIME AND PLACE OF TRIAL, JURY TRIAL SET FOR SEP-19-2022 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 220. FOR JURY TRIAL. THE COURT ORDERS COUNSEL TO MEET AND CONFER ON THE FOLLOWING JOINT TIME ESTIMATES; MOTIONS IN LIMINE (COUNSEL TO PREPARE BINDER WITH MOTIONS AND OPPOSITIONS (ONLY MOTIONS COUNSEL CANNOT AGREE ON)); EXHIBITS; VERDICT FORM; QUESTIONNAIRE; COURT TRIAL OR JURY TRIAL; AND JOINT JURY INSTRUCTIONS. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SEIU LOCAL 87'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. CONTINUED TO MARCH 8, 2019. DEFENDANT HAS LEAVE TO FILE A BRIEF OF NO MORE THAN 5 PAGES EXPLAINING WHETHER SECTION 301 OF THE LMRA PREEMPTS PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS. PLEASE FOCUS ON SECTION 301 CASELAW THAT INVOLVES FEHA, CFRA AND/OR FMLA CLAIMS AS MOST CASES HAVE NOT FOUND PREEMPTION IN THE FEHA CONTEXT. (SEE RAMIREZ V. FOX TELEVISION STATION, INC. (1993) 998 F.2D 743, 748.) DEFENDANT'S BRIEF SHALL BE FILED AND SERVED BY FEBRUARY 27, 2019. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO FILE A RESPONDING BRIEF OF NO MORE THAN 5 PAGES BY MARCH 4, 2019. ALL PARTIES TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES OF THEIR FILINGS WITH DEPARTMENT 302. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: W. Frederick Geonetta, Esq. 510-254-3776, Jennifer Langston, Esq. 510-254-3778 of Geonetta & Frucht LLP for Plaintiff.

Parties argues race discrimination, retaliation preemtion, punitive damages. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT SEIU LOCAL 87's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

Taken Under Submission.*** (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-22-2019 Continued To Mar-08-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SEIU LOCAL 87's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

CONTINUED to March 8, 2019. Defendant has leave to file a brief of no more than 5 pages explaining whether section 301 of the LMRA preempts plaintiff's claims. Please focus on section 301 caselaw that involves FEHA, CFRA and/or FMLA claims as most cases have not found preemption in the FEHA context. (See Ramirez v. Fox Television Station, Inc. (1993) 998 F.2d 743, 748.) Defendant's brief shall be filed and served by February 27, 2019. Plaintiff has leave to file a responding brief of no more than 5 pages by March 4, 2019. All parties to provide courtesy copies of their filings with Department 302.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600 email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
